“Not In Our Name”: Jewish protesters join 500,000 in London to mark 77 years since the Nakba.  London saw a massive outpouring of support for Palestine today, as approximately half a million people gathered to protest Israel’s ongoing military campaign in Gaza and mark the 77th anniversary of the Nakba, when Palestinians were forcibly displaced from their homes. Reporting from the scene, MEE’s Ava Warriner observed a diverse crowd united in solidarity. Among the demonstrators was a Jewish protester who pointed to the visible Jewish presence at the march and stated they were there to say: “Not in our name,” emphasising that standing with Palestinians is not just an act of solidarity, but a reflection of shared humanity and justice. “Our safety is connected - none of us are free until Palestine is free.”
